title: The recipe card my aunt asked me to copy
summary: Describe an old, fading handwritten recipe and the afternoon you spent copying it out so it would not be lost.
topic: Copying an old handwritten recipe before it fades
prompt: Write 9–11 sentences narrating an afternoon when you copied an old handwritten recipe (or letter, or note) so it would not be lost. Describe what the paper looked like, what you did, and why it mattered. Use past simple, 'so', and 'because'.
hintStarters: My aunt handed me the card and said it was the only…
The ink had faded so much that I could barely…
I copied each line slowly because I was afraid…
When I finished, she put the new card in the drawer and…

hintVocab.meaningVi: khó khăn lắm mới, hầu như không

sampleText: Last winter my aunt gave me a small card and asked me to copy it. It was the recipe for the soup she cooked every New Year. The card was old and soft, and the corners were torn. The ink had faded so much that I could barely read some of the words. There was a brown stain near the bottom, probably from the soup itself. I sat at the kitchen table with a new card and a pen. I copied each line slowly because I was afraid of making a mistake. Some numbers were hard to read, so I asked my aunt to check them. She remembered most of them by heart. When the card said 'a pinch of salt,' she laughed and told me her mother had written that, not her. I wrote the words carefully and did not change anything, even the small mistakes. I did not want to lose her mother's voice in the recipe. When I finished, my aunt read the new card and smiled. She put the old card and the new one together in the drawer. She said one day I would pass them down too. I keep a photo of the old card on my phone now, because paper does not last, but I want to remember how it looked.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The ink had faded so much that I could barely read
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mực đã phai đến mức tôi khó khăn lắm mới đọc được

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a brown stain near the bottom
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một vết ố nâu gần phía dưới
tôi chép từng dòng một cách chậm rãi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: remembered most of them by heart
lose her mother's voice in the recipe
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đánh mất giọng nói của mẹ bà trong công thức

sampleAnnotations.phrase: put the old card and the new one together in the drawer
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đặt tấm thẻ cũ và tấm mới cùng nhau trong ngăn kéo
nhớ nó trông như thế nào
viết các từ một cách cẩn thận

sampleGrammarNotes.title: so … that for result
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'The ink had faded so much that I could barely read some of the words' dùng cấu trúc 'so + tính từ/trạng từ + that' để diễn tả kết quả: mực phai nhiều đến mức gây ra hậu quả là khó đọc.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'The ink had faded so much that I could barely read some of the words' uses 'so + much + that' to show a result: the fading was great enough to cause the consequence of being hard to read.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: because for reason
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Câu 'I copied each line slowly because I was afraid of making a mistake' dùng 'because' để nối hành động với lý do của nó.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: The sentence 'I copied each line slowly because I was afraid of making a mistake' uses 'because' to link the action to its reason.

sampleTranslationVi: Mùa đông năm ngoái dì tôi đưa tôi một tấm thẻ nhỏ và nhờ tôi chép lại. Đó là công thức nấu món súp mà bà nấu mỗi dịp năm mới. Tấm thẻ cũ và mềm, các góc bị rách. Mực đã phai đến mức tôi khó khăn lắm mới đọc được vài chữ. Có một vết ố nâu gần phía dưới, chắc là từ chính món súp. Tôi ngồi ở bàn bếp với một tấm thẻ mới và một cây bút. Tôi chép từng dòng chậm rãi vì sợ mắc lỗi. Vài con số khó đọc, nên tôi nhờ dì kiểm tra. Bà thuộc lòng hầu hết chúng. Khi tấm thẻ ghi 'một nhúm muối,' bà cười và nói mẹ bà đã viết câu đó, không phải bà. Tôi viết các từ thật cẩn thận và không thay đổi điều gì, kể cả những lỗi nhỏ. Tôi không muốn đánh mất giọng nói của mẹ bà trong công thức. Khi tôi viết xong, dì đọc tấm thẻ mới và mỉm cười. Bà đặt tấm thẻ cũ và tấm mới cùng nhau trong ngăn kéo. Bà nói một ngày nào đó tôi cũng sẽ truyền chúng lại. Giờ tôi giữ một bức ảnh của tấm thẻ cũ trong điện thoại, vì giấy không bền lâu, nhưng tôi muốn nhớ nó trông như thế nào.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best-written sentence describing how worn the card was.
options: The card was old and soft, and the corners were teared.
The card was old and soft, and the corners were torn.
The card was olds and soft, and the corners were torn.
The card was old and soft, and the corners were broke.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Torn' is the correct past participle of 'tear'. 'Teared' is wrong; 'olds' is not a plural adjective; 'broke' is wrong for paper edges.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the past participle of 'tear'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that uses 'so … that' correctly for a result.
options: The ink had faded so much what I could barely read it.
The ink had faded too much that I could barely read it.
The ink had faded so much that I could barely read it.
The ink had faded so that much I could barely read it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'So much that' links degree to result. 'What' is wrong; 'too much that' is not the structure; word order in the last option is broken.
mcQuestions.hint: Which linking word follows 'so much' to show a result?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence gives the reason correctly with 'because'?
options: I copied each line slowly because of I was afraid of making a mistake.
I copied each line slowly because I was afraid of make a mistake.
I copied each line slowly, because afraid of a mistake.
I copied each line slowly because I was afraid of making a mistake.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because + subject + verb' is correct, and 'afraid of making' uses the gerund. 'Because of' needs a noun; 'of make' is wrong; the last option drops the subject.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'afraid of', should the verb be 'make' or 'making'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that says she knew the recipe without reading it.
options: She remembered most of them by heart.
She remembered most of them by the heart.
She remembered most of them in heart.
She remembered most of them from heart.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'By heart' is the fixed idiom for knowing something from memory. The other prepositions and the article are incorrect.
mcQuestions.hint: What is the fixed phrase meaning 'from memory'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence about not altering the text.
options: I did not changed anything, even the small mistakes.
I did not change anything, even the small mistakes.
I did not change nothing, even the small mistakes.
I not changed anything, even the small mistakes.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'did not' use the base verb 'change' with 'anything'. 'Changed' is a double past; 'nothing' is a double negative; 'I not changed' omits the auxiliary.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'did not', is the verb in base or past form?

options: She said one day I would pass down them too.
She said one day I would pass they down too.
She said one day I would pass them down too.
She said one day I would passed them down too.
mcQuestions.explanation: With a pronoun object, the phrasal verb separates: 'pass them down'. 'Pass down them' is unnatural; 'they' must be object 'them'; 'would passed' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does a pronoun go in a separable phrasal verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the sentence that best describes a mark left by food.
options: There was a brown stained near the bottom.
There were a brown stain near the bottom.
There was a brown stain near to the bottom.
There was a brown stain near the bottom.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'A brown stain' (noun) with singular 'was' is correct. 'Stained' is the adjective/past form; 'were' disagrees; 'near to' is unnatural here.
mcQuestions.hint: Do you need the noun 'stain' or the form 'stained'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence that explains why she kept a photo.
options: I keep a photo because paper does not last.
I keep a photo because paper not last.
I keep a photo because of paper does not last.
I keep a photo, because paper doesn't lasts.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because paper does not last' is correct. 'Not last' omits the auxiliary; 'because of' needs a noun; 'doesn't lasts' double-marks the verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Does 'paper' take 'does not last' or 'doesn't lasts'?

options: I could barely to read some of the words.
I could barely read some of the words.
I could barely reading some of the words.
I barely could not read some of the words.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Could barely read' uses the base verb after the modal. 'To read' and 'reading' are wrong after 'could'; the last option makes a double negative.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best sentence about feeling worried while writing.
options: I was afraid making a mistake.
I was afraid of make a mistake.
I was afraid of making a mistake.
I was afraid to making a mistake.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Afraid of + gerund' (making) is the correct pattern. 'Afraid making' drops the preposition; 'of make' uses the base verb; 'to making' mixes two forms.
mcQuestions.hint: What follows 'afraid of': a gerund or a base verb?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is it more important to preserve the exact words of something old, even its mistakes, or to fix it so it is easier for the next person to use? Why?
